title: Cracca brevipes
taxonomy: Cracca brevipes is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). It is native to Australia, where it is found in the eastern and southern regions. The species is a climber, using its tendrils to attach itself to nearby surfaces. Cracca brevipes is classified in the genus Cracca, which is a group of flowering plants known for their small, pea-like flowers. The plant's classification is further broken down into the family Fabaceae, subfamily Faboideae, and tribe Phaseoleae.
